verses.text: God bids Jeremiah write all His words on a roll; Baruch writes them at his dictation. The word is recorded. God preserves His message in writing for a people who would not listen.
God bids Jeremiah write all His words on a roll; Baruch writes them at his dictation. The word is recorded. God preserves His message in writing for a people who would not listen.
God bids Jeremiah write all His words on a roll; Baruch writes them at his dictation. The word is recorded. God preserves His message in writing for a people who would not listen.
God bids Jeremiah write all His words on a roll; Baruch writes them at his dictation. The word is recorded. God preserves His message in writing for a people who would not listen.
Jeremiah, shut up, sends Baruch to read the roll in the temple on the fast day. The word goes forth by another. When one mouth is stopped, God still spreads His truth.
Jeremiah, shut up, sends Baruch to read the roll in the temple on the fast day. The word goes forth by another. When one mouth is stopped, God still spreads His truth.
Jeremiah, shut up, sends Baruch to read the roll in the temple on the fast day. The word goes forth by another. When one mouth is stopped, God still spreads His truth.
Jeremiah, shut up, sends Baruch to read the roll in the temple on the fast day. The word goes forth by another. When one mouth is stopped, God still spreads His truth.
Baruch reads to the people; the princes hear and, afraid, report it to the king. The word stirs fear. God's word still pierces those who hear it honestly.
Baruch reads to the people; the princes hear and, afraid, report it to the king. The word stirs fear. God's word still pierces those who hear it honestly.
Baruch reads to the people; the princes hear and, afraid, report it to the king. The word stirs fear. God's word still pierces those who hear it honestly.
Baruch reads to the people; the princes hear and, afraid, report it to the king. The word stirs fear. God's word still pierces those who hear it honestly.
Baruch reads to the people; the princes hear and, afraid, report it to the king. The word stirs fear. God's word still pierces those who hear it honestly.
The princes have Baruch read again and are alarmed, resolving to tell the king. They take it seriously. Some, at least, tremble at the word of God.
The princes have Baruch read again and are alarmed, resolving to tell the king. They take it seriously. Some, at least, tremble at the word of God.
The princes have Baruch read again and are alarmed, resolving to tell the king. They take it seriously. Some, at least, tremble at the word of God.
They ask how the roll was written and counsel Baruch and Jeremiah to hide. They sense danger. The faithful sometimes need protection from a hostile power.
They ask how the roll was written and counsel Baruch and Jeremiah to hide. They sense danger. The faithful sometimes need protection from a hostile power.
They ask how the roll was written and counsel Baruch and Jeremiah to hide. They sense danger. The faithful sometimes need protection from a hostile power.
The king Jehoiakim cuts the roll with a penknife and burns it piece by piece in the fire. He defies God's word. Men may burn the page, but they cannot unsay the word of God.
The king Jehoiakim cuts the roll with a penknife and burns it piece by piece in the fire. He defies God's word. Men may burn the page, but they cannot unsay the word of God.
The king Jehoiakim cuts the roll with a penknife and burns it piece by piece in the fire. He defies God's word. Men may burn the page, but they cannot unsay the word of God.
The king Jehoiakim cuts the roll with a penknife and burns it piece by piece in the fire. He defies God's word. Men may burn the page, but they cannot unsay the word of God.

Neither the king nor his servants were afraid nor tore their clothes; he ordered Jeremiah and Baruch seized, but 'the LORD hid them.' Hardness met the word. God shielded His servants from the king's hand.

Neither the king nor his servants were afraid nor tore their clothes; he ordered Jeremiah and Baruch seized, but 'the LORD hid them.' Hardness met the word. God shielded His servants from the king's hand.

Neither the king nor his servants were afraid nor tore their clothes; he ordered Jeremiah and Baruch seized, but 'the LORD hid them.' Hardness met the word. God shielded His servants from the king's hand.
God bids another roll be written; Jehoiakim shall have no heir on the throne, and his body cast out. Burning the word brings judgment. To destroy God's message is to seal one's doom.

God bids another roll be written; Jehoiakim shall have no heir on the throne, and his body cast out. Burning the word brings judgment. To destroy God's message is to seal one's doom.
Jeremiah dictates a new roll, and Baruch writes all again, 'and there were added... many like words.' The word returns fuller. What men destroy, God restores and enlarges.
